title: PayPal Webhooks (incomplete-doc fail-closed sample)
overview: PayPal delivers webhook events as HTTPS POSTs to listener URLs you subscribe (up to 10 per app). The provided official pages state the event names, the receiver-response/retry rules (respond 2xx; otherwise PayPal retries up to 25 times over 3 days), and that authenticity is verified via a signature — but they DEFER the signature headers/algorithm/steps to a separate Integration guide and do NOT document the JSON payload field schema. This sample is intentionally incomplete to exercise fail-closed behaviour: the missing base URL, signature details, and payload fields must be surfaced (missing / unverified), never fabricated.

endpoints.summary: PAYMENT.SALE.COMPLETED webhook. Delivered as an HTTPS POST to your subscribed listener URL when a sale completes.
PAYMENT.CAPTURE.COMPLETED webhook. Delivered as an HTTPS POST to your subscribed listener URL when a payment capture completes.
CHECKOUT.ORDER.APPROVED webhook. Delivered as an HTTPS POST to your subscribed listener URL when a buyer approved a checkout order.

schemas.name: WebhookEvent

fields.name: id
fields.description: The ID of the webhook event notification.

fields.name: resource_type
fields.description: The name of the resource related to the webhook notification event.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: A summary description for the event notification.

fields.name: resource
fields.description: The resource that triggered the webhook event notification.

fields.name: create_time
fields.description: The date and time when the webhook event notification was created.

schemas.source: PayPal Webhooks REST API reference — webhook-event object (payload field schema NOT present in the provided overview/event-name pages)

operational.detail: Webhooks are HTTPS POSTs from PayPal to listener URLs you subscribe (up to 10 per app); messages are posted to every subscribed listener URL.

Authenticity is verified either by computing the CRC32 of the message and verifying its signature, or by posting the message + stored webhook ID + header information back to PayPal's verify-signature endpoint. The exact request headers, signing algorithm, and verification steps are NOT on the provided pages — deferred to the Integration guide.
Respond with HTTP 200 or other 2xx. If the endpoint does not respond successfully, PayPal reattempts delivery up to 25 times over the course of 3 days.

missing: No base URL / host for the webhooks management API is stated on the provided pages; OpenAPI servers are intentionally empty (delivery is to a subscriber-configured listener URL).
The signature verification request headers, the signing algorithm, and the verification steps are deferred to the PayPal Integration guide (not provided) — left unspecified rather than inferred.
The full JSON payload field schema for each event is NOT documented on the provided pages (the event-names page explicitly says developers must consult separate API reference docs).
The webhook-subscription management endpoints (create/list/delete subscriptions) are alluded to ('up to 10 URLs may be subscribed') but no path, method, request, or response is given on these pages.
